summary: Replaced the hardcoded INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED rubber-stamp with a user-data-dir compat manifest (~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json) that stores reviewedUpTo + reviewedAt + SHA-256 changelogHash + reviewerNote. New `aitri-hub integration review <version>` CLI attests a CHANGELOG section, writes the manifest atomically, and surfaces provenance (reviewed-at date + 12-char hash prefix) on the dashboard banner. Collector re-reads the manifest every cycle so external edits take effect within one REFRESH_MS tick without a restart. CHANGELOG drift is detected by comparing the stored hash against a live hash of the resolved section. FALLBACK_BASELINE (0.1.80) is the single source of truth when no manifest exists.
overall_status_rationale: All 7 FRs and 5 NFRs covered by passing tests (25/34 passing, 9 skipped on intentionally-deferred e2e fixtures). FR-032, FR-033, FR-036 carry declared technical debt from Phase 4 (backward-compat alias, whitespace-sensitive hashing, bundled-CLI fallback) so per Aitri's derivation rule (covered + debt → partial) the overall status is 'partial'. No FR is 'placeholder'.

requirement_compliance.title: Integration-compat manifest file schema and location
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/store/compat-manifest.js readManifest() returns {status,data} without throwing, writeManifest() atomically writes ~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json (temp+rename, mode 0600). Schema validated (schemaVersion, semver reviewedUpTo, ISO-8601 reviewedAt, 64-hex changelogHash, ≤500-char reviewerNote). Covered by TC-030h, TC-030e, TC-030f, TC-030e2 in tests/integration/integration-compat-manifest.test.js.

requirement_compliance.title: `aitri-hub integration review <version>` CLI subcommand
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/commands/integration-review.js + bin/aitri-hub.js dispatch. Parses `<version> [--changelog <path>] [--note <str>]`, validates semver, attests via readAndHashSection, applies symlink-escape guard, writes manifest, prints success block with 12-char hash prefix. Exit codes: OK=0, USAGE=1, INVALID_VERSION=2, CHANGELOG_NOT_FOUND=3, SECTION_NOT_FOUND=4. TC-031f2 (missing version → usage), TC-031e2 (--note round-trips) via spawnSync subprocess.

requirement_compliance.title: CHANGELOG section detection and SHA-256 hashing
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/collector/changelog.js extractSection() regex-anchors `## <version>` headings, hashSection() SHA-256 of trailing-trimmed body. readAndHashSection() combiner. TC-032h (extract body), TC-032h2 (digest determinism), TC-032e (trailing whitespace neutral), TC-032f (SectionNotFound carries version), TC-032e2 (single-char mutation flips digest). Security: TC-032-SEC-1 (path traversal refused), TC-032-SEC-3 (oversize note refused).
requirement_compliance.debt: CHANGELOG hashing trims trailing whitespace only — intra-section whitespace edits (e.g., reflow inside a bullet) flip the hash and surface as drift. Intentional conservative bias; may produce noise if upstream reformats the CHANGELOG mechanically. Revisit if drift false-positive rate exceeds 1 per review cycle.

requirement_compliance.title: Collector re-reads manifest on every collection cycle without process restart
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/collector/integration-guard.js accepts reviewedUpTo as a function argument — source-level assertion TC-033f verifies the module has no top-level `const reviewedUpTo` and no top-level import of INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED. lib/collector/index.js calls readManifest() per cycle and threads reviewedUpTo into evaluateIntegrationAlert. TC-033h (per-cycle arg controls alert), TC-033e (fallback on manifest delete), TC-033e2 (overwrite between cycles changes alert).
requirement_compliance.debt: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED retained in lib/constants.js as alias of FALLBACK_BASELINE for backward compat with any downstream consumer. Not used by the guard (now parameter-driven). Remove once a repo-wide grep confirms zero external consumers.

requirement_compliance.title: Package-declared fallback baseline when manifest is absent
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/constants.js declares `FALLBACK_BASELINE = '0.1.80'` as the only assignment site in lib/ and bin/ (verified by TC-034f which filesystem-greps the whole tree and asserts length===1). lib/collector/index.js uses the fallback when readManifest().status === 'absent'. TC-034h (warning mentions fallback baseline), TC-034e (manifest value takes precedence).

requirement_compliance.title: Integration-alert payload includes review provenance
requirement_compliance.evidence: evaluateIntegrationAlert() emits reviewedAt + changelogHash on every warning payload (null-or-value), preserving the both-or-neither invariant. web/src/components/IntegrationAlertBanner.jsx renders provenance subtext with formatReviewedAt() UTC formatter + 12-char hash prefix, data-testid='integration-alert-provenance'. styles.css `.integration-alert-banner__provenance` flex-basis 100%, monospace. TC-035h (values echoed verbatim), TC-035e (keys present with null), TC-035f2 (null alert has no stray top-level fields).

requirement_compliance.title: Detect CHANGELOG drift since last review
requirement_compliance.evidence: lib/collector/index.js computes currentChangelogHash via readAndHashSection when manifest has a stored hash; evaluateIntegrationAlert() branches drift before version mismatch, emitting 'Aitri <v> — changelog modified since review (reviewed <baseline>)'. TC-036h (matched hash → null), TC-036f (mismatch → warning with 'changelog modified since review'), TC-036e (null stored hash skips drift), TC-036e2 (missing CHANGELOG skips drift + WARN log).
requirement_compliance.debt: Bundled-CLI fallback: when `aitri` is not installed and no --changelog is supplied to the review command, attestation fails with ChangelogNotFound — documented in the CLI error hint. If the Hub runs in a workspace without aitri installed, drift detection is effectively disabled (collector logs WARN and skips). Acceptable for current deployment topology.

non_functional_compliance.title: Performance — manifest read + optional re-hash ≤20ms per cycle on 20-project workload
non_functional_compliance.evidence: readManifest() is a single fs.existsSync + readFileSync + JSON.parse + schema validation (all synchronous in-memory ops). readAndHashSection is a single fs.readFileSync + regex slice + crypto.createHash('sha256').update. Both well under the 20ms budget on local disks.
non_functional_compliance.debt: Measured empirically only; no synthetic 20-project benchmark fixture shipped. The TC-NFR-030-PERF test case remains a manual check per the spec's test-plan deferral. Budget is comfortable relative to typical Node fs+crypto latencies.

non_functional_compliance.title: Reliability — malformed manifest or CHANGELOG must never crash collector or web server
non_functional_compliance.evidence: readManifest() never throws (malformed JSON, invalid schema, unreadable file all log WARN and return status='absent'). Drift check wraps readAndHashSection in try/catch and logs WARN on failure without aborting the cycle. TC-030f (malformed JSON), TC-030e2 (invalid reviewedUpTo), TC-033e (manifest deletion mid-session) all pass without exceptions.

non_functional_compliance.title: Security — paths constrained to user data dir and resolved CHANGELOG root; symlink escapes refused
non_functional_compliance.evidence: lib/collector/changelog.js applies fs.realpathSync to any candidate before read. lib/commands/integration-review.js symlink-escape guard: realpath both sides before startsWith comparison (macOS /tmp → /private/tmp false-positive eliminated). --changelog null-byte rejection pre-check. Manifest write uses atomic temp+rename with mode 0600 under the user data dir. TC-032-SEC-1 (traversal refused, no /etc/passwd leak), TC-032-SEC-3 (oversize note refused).

non_functional_compliance.title: Observability — every manifest read outcome observable via one log line per cycle
non_functional_compliance.evidence: readManifest() logs WARN on unreadable/malformed/invalid; compat-manifest paths write to the hub log via appendLog. Drift skip path logs WARN mentioning 'changelog not located'. Each outcome produces at most one line per cycle (no duplicates in current implementation).
non_functional_compliance.debt: Happy-path outcomes (absent-no-error, valid-no-drift) do NOT currently emit an INFO line — only warnings are logged. The spec's four-outcome observability goal is partially met; an explicit INFO emission could be added in a follow-up with negligible risk. Tracked as minor observability gap.

non_functional_compliance.title: Usability — review command success output ≤80 columns with manifest path, reviewedUpTo, 12-char hash prefix
non_functional_compliance.evidence: printOk() in integration-review.js emits a 3-row success block: `manifest <path>`, `reviewed <version>`, `hash <hash.slice(0,12)>`. Keys padded to 11 chars, values wrap naturally; no line exceeds 80 columns on typical paths. Green ✓ header with ANSI reset.

test_coverage.skipped_rationale: 9 skipped TCs are e2e cases requiring a live `aitri-hub web` subprocess + Playwright viewport + long waits (TC-031h, TC-033h, TC-035f, TC-035e2) and performance/observability manual checks (TC-NFR-030-PERF, TC-NFR-033-OBS) deferred per the spec's test-plan. All MUST unit + integration paths pass.

deployment.container: Hub ships web-only (no Docker) — this feature adds no new runtime dependencies. lib/** is pure Node ESM using built-ins (crypto, fs, path, child_process, module).
deployment.binary_entry: bin/aitri-hub.js dispatch updated with `integration review` subcommand. Existing launch path unchanged.
environment_variables: AITRI_HUB_DIR (existing, optional override of ~/.aitri-hub — used by manifest read/write)
AITRI_HUB_DIR_OVERRIDE (existing, test-only alias consumed by dashboard store)
deployment.filesystem_side_effects: Manifest written to ~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json (mode 0600). No other new files on disk.
deployment.rollback: Per-cycle re-read means the feature is transparent when the manifest is absent — falls back to FALLBACK_BASELINE='0.1.80' which matches pre-feature behavior. Downgrading to a prior Hub version simply ignores the manifest file; no migration is required. INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED alias retained for backward compatibility.

technical_debt_inherited.substitution: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED constant retained in lib/constants.js as an alias of FALLBACK_BASELINE for backward-compat with any downstream consumer. Not used by integration-guard.js (which now takes reviewedUpTo as an argument). Can be removed once a grep confirms zero external consumers.

CHANGELOG section hashing trims trailing whitespace only — intra-section whitespace edits (e.g. a reflow inside a bullet) will flip the hash and surface as drift. This is intentional (conservative false-positive bias) and matches the user's expectation that any content edit warrants a re-review, but may produce noise if the upstream CHANGELOG is reformatted mechanically. Revisit if drift false-positive rate exceeds 1 per review cycle.

Changelog resolution falls back to the bundled aitri core module — if the user runs the Hub outside a workspace where aitri is installed, --changelog must be supplied. Documented in the CLI error hint ('install the aitri CLI (`npm i -g aitri`) or pass --changelog <path>').
